title: Summary of Central Government Operations, Iran, 1962-1973 (World Bank, 1974)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank); data compiled by the Bank from Bank Markazi Annual Reports, IMF Reports, and Monetary Accounts
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Source document is data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1974_economic_development_vol4_statistical_appendix.pdf (Iran - Economic Development, Vol. 4 of 4: Statistical appendix -- internally titled 'Volume III: Statistical Appendix' -- dated October 14 1974). Table 6.1 is on PDF page 54 (printed page number '-46-'). Rendered at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -r 300 -f 54 -l 54 -png` and read directly from the rendered PNG using the Read tool; this page's print quality was excellent (crisp typewriter print, no fading/smudging issues seen elsewhere in this session's older 1971 documents) so no further crop/zoom passes were needed and every digit was read with high confidence on the first pass. Cross-checked via two independent internal identities that both closed EXACTLY for all 12 columns: (a) Savings = Total Revenues - Current Expenditure (e.g. 1971: 258.3-176.7=81.6, matching printed Savings 81.6; 1972 prelim: 301.8-218.3=83.5, matching; 1973 budget: 361.6-290.9=70.7, matching); (b) Deficit = Capital Expenditure - Savings (e.g. 1971: 115.1-81.6=33.5, matching printed Deficit 33.5; 1973 budget: 179.8-70.7=109.1, matching). A third identity was also confirmed: Discrepancy = (Net borrowing abroad + Net borrowing from banking system + Nonbank holdings of Government Securities) - Deficit, e.g. 1969: (21.3+25.3-4.5)-46.8=-4.7, matching printed Discrepancy -4.7 exactly; 1965: (0.1+3.4+1.1)-9.4=-4.8, matching printed -4.8 exactly. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated. 'n.a.' cells (Net borrowing from banking system and its three sub-components, Budget Estimates 1973 column) are as printed in the original.
time_coverage: 1962-1973 (fiscal years ending March 20; last two columns are Preliminary Actuals 1972 and Budget Estimates 1973)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source: Table 6.1, PDF page 54 ('-46-' in original pagination) of the Statistical Appendix, footnoted 'Treasury General and Plan and Budget Organization' (footnote 1/). This is the single master budget table of the report -- the overall central government fiscal balance (revenue, current + capital expenditure, savings, deficit, and full financing breakdown by external borrowing, banking-system borrowing, and nonbank bond holdings) -- and is a LATER, REVISED, and EXTENDED vintage of the same underlying series covered by the already-extracted wb1971-ordinary-budget-central-govt-expenditure-1962-1972 and wb1971-government-revenue-summary-1962-1972 datasets (source: a different 1971 World Bank report using fiscal-year labels e.g. '1970/71'), so the two vintages should NOT be assumed identical -- e.g. this 1974 report's Total Revenue for '1971' is 258.3 vs the 1971 report's Draft Budget figure of 241.8 for '1971/72', reflecting both a slightly different fiscal-year convention and normal between-report revisions. Critically, this table extends the series into 1972 (Preliminary Actuals) and 1973 (Budget Estimates), the two years immediately before the October 1973 oil-price shock's full budgetary impact hit (Total Revenue nearly doubled from 173.4 in 1970 to 361.6 in the 1973 budget estimate, with Oil revenue's share of total revenue rising from ~49% in 1970 to ~62% in the 1973 estimate) -- this is the first primary-source budget data in this project's collection to reach into the early-1970s oil-boom years. Companion tables in the same document not yet extracted (flagged for future work): Table 6.2 'Central Government Revenues' (PDF page 55, more granular than this table's Oil/Non-Oil split), Table 6.3 'Central Government Current Expenditures' (PDF page 56, functional breakdown analogous to the already-extracted 1971 Table 5.1), Table 6.4 'Development Expenditures of the Plan Organization in the Third and Fourth Plans' (PDF page 57), Table 6.5 'Government Securities by Type of Security and Holder' (PDF page 58), Table 7.1 'Movements in Monetary Aggregates' (PDF page 60, extracted separately as dataset wb1974-movements-monetary-aggregates-1966-1973), Table 7.2 'Consolidated Balance Sheet of the Banking System' (PDF page 61), and Table 7.3 'Summary of the Balance Sheet of Bank Markazi Iran' (PDF page 62) plus further banking tables (commercial bank reserves/liquidity, discount rate, banking unit distribution, interest rates, credit by industry) through roughly PDF page 70. Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era budget/money-supply/banking data from primary-source World Bank PDFs in data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/, following the same visual-verification methodology as the wb1960-* and wb1971-* datasets in this same directory.
